options.label: A
options.text: A force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact

options.label: B
options.text: A force that always makes objects move faster

options.label: C
options.text: A force that only acts on smooth surfaces

options.label: D
options.text: A force that pulls objects towards the Earth

explanation: Friction is a contact force that resists the movement of objects sliding past each other. It acts opposite to the direction of motion and is stronger on rough surfaces than smooth ones.
